description: Trigger discrimination for the external API/event CONTRACT (this skill) vs the internal audit record (audit-log-architect) and authorization design (authorization-matrix-designer), plus the newer colliders that already name it: the internal streaming backbone (streaming-event-architect), live client push (realtime-subscription-architect), the internal write-path gateway (command-gateway-architect), the analytics event schema (event-schema-architect), notification/webhook UX (notification-webhook-ux-designer), and the generated reference docs for this contract (api-doc-generator-designer).

cases.prompt: Design the webhook events and payload schemas integrations subscribe to when workspace records change, including retries and signing.
cases.reason: Externally consumed event contracts with delivery semantics — the integration surface.

cases.prompt: Keep an append-only, tenant-scoped history of every permission change and data export that auditors can rely on.
cases.reason: Durable internal record with integrity guarantees — audit, not a notification feed.

cases.prompt: Decide which permissions exist and which roles hold them — including what a read-only role can touch.
cases.reason: The permission matrix itself; the API skill only binds an existing matrix into token scopes.

cases.prompt: Design how our billing, inventory, and notification services exchange events internally — topics, partition keys, consumer-failure handling, and replay after an outage.
cases.reason: Internal service-to-service transport — topics, partitions, DLQ, replay — is streaming-event-architect. This skill owns only the external contract offered to integrators, not the internal data-pipeline that feeds it.

cases.prompt: Clients should get live updates over WebSocket when a shared resource changes — subscribe-time authorization, cross-node fan-out, and reconnect that replays what they missed.
cases.reason: Live push to client connections (subscribe-time authz, fan-out, reconnect/replay) is realtime-subscription-architect. This skill owns request/response APIs and server-to-server webhook delivery, not the live client channel.

cases.prompt: Every protected mutation should go through one server-side path that authenticates the actor, derives tenant scope, dedups retries, executes, and emits an audit record — with no client able to write around it.
cases.reason: The internal mediated write pipeline and its no-bypass invariant are command-gateway-architect. This skill defines the external contract those endpoints expose, not the internal dispatch path behind it.

cases.prompt: Define our analytics tracking plan: consistent event names, typed properties with enums, the global properties every event carries, and how anonymous events stitch to identified users.
cases.reason: Behavioral analytics events for measurement (tracking plan, property schema, identity stitching) are event-schema-architect. This skill's events are an external contract offered to integrators, not a measurement schema.

cases.prompt: Design the notification preferences center and the developer webhook dashboard — per-category channel controls, a delivery log with replay, and secret-rotation UX.
cases.reason: The human/developer UX over webhooks (preferences, delivery-log dashboard, rotation affordances) is notification-webhook-ux-designer. This skill defines the wire delivery contract those screens surface, not the UI.

cases.prompt: Generate our API reference from the schema so it stays in sync, with validated examples and a version selector.
cases.reason: Generating drift-proof API reference from the source of truth is api-doc-generator-designer. This skill designs the contract itself; the generator only documents it.
